title: erigeron basalticus
intro: Erigeron basalticus, also known as alpine fleabane, is a perennial herb that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erigeron basalticus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and relieving symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
